evals.name: Generate full paper outline from one-liner
evals.prompt: I want to write a paper about multi-task reinforcement learning using dynamic task routing. The core idea is to let agents dynamically select which modules to use during execution. I'm targeting ICLR and I have preliminary experiments on 3 continuous control benchmarks. Please generate a complete paper outline with section guidance.
evals.expected_output: Complete 7-section outline (Abstract through Conclusion) with recommended structure, key points, and evaluation checklist for each section
expectations: Outline includes all 7 sections (Abstract, Intro, Related Work, Methodology, Experiments, Discussion, Conclusion)
Each section has recommended structure (subsections or key parts)
Each section has 3+ key points to cover
Each section has a purpose statement
Intro section includes subsections for Hook, Gap Analysis, Core Idea, Contributions, and Organization
Related Work section shows how to organize by research direction
Experiments section includes Setup, Main Results, Ablation, and Analysis
Total estimated page count is reasonable (8-10 pages for method paper)
Citations expectations are provided (e.g., 10 for intro, 20 for related work)

evals.name: Generate detailed Introduction section with examples
evals.prompt: Generate a detailed Introduction section draft for a paper about reward models for math problem solving. The paper proposes a new approach to training reward models that better captures mathematical correctness. We're comparing against existing reward model training methods. Please provide sample text for each subsection (Hook, Background, Gap, Our Idea, Contributions, Paper Organization), along with writing guidance.
evals.expected_output: Detailed Introduction draft with sample text for each subsection, citations marked with [CITE-X], key points to cover, and common mistakes to avoid
expectations: Introduction has 5 subsections: Hook, Background, Gap Analysis, Core Idea, Contributions, Paper Organization
Each subsection includes sample/template text
Citations are marked with [CITE-1], [CITE-2], etc.
Hook includes a specific example or problem statement
Gap Analysis identifies 2-3 limitations of existing approaches
Core Idea is expressed in clear, non-technical language
Contributions are numbered and specific (not vague)
Paper Organization section lists the main sections
Common mistakes for Introduction are listed (e.g., 'too much detail', 'unclear gap')
Quality checklist is provided for evaluating Introduction

evals.name: Provide writing guidance for Related Work section
evals.prompt: I need help structuring the Related Work section for my paper on multi-agent reinforcement learning. My paper proposes a new coordination mechanism. The key related areas are: (1) Communication protocols in MARL, (2) Centralized training with decentralized execution, (3) Attention mechanisms for agent interaction. How should I organize and what should I include?
evals.expected_output: Structured guidance for organizing Related Work into 3 research directions, with recommendations for papers to discuss, how to position each direction relative to the proposed work, and evaluation criteria
expectations: Recommends organizing by research direction (not chronologically)
Suggests 3-5 representative papers per direction
Provides guidance on how to position each direction relative to proposed work
Includes sample structure for each research direction
Recommends number of citations (e.g., 20-30 for Related Work)
Common mistakes in Related Work are listed
Checklist for evaluating Related Work is provided
Guidance includes how to avoid strawman criticism of prior work
Specific language suggestions for positioning ('differs in that...', 'complements prior work...')

evals.name: Generate Methodology section with formal definitions
evals.prompt: Help me structure the Methodology section for a paper on reinforcement learning with multi-task routing. I need to define the problem formally, explain my framework, and provide algorithm details. The key technical contribution is a learned routing network that selects task-specific modules. Please provide a template with placeholders for my specific technical content.
evals.expected_output: Structured Methodology section template with Problem Definition, Framework Overview, Technical Details, and Training Procedure, including where to put equations, algorithms, and key technical details
expectations: Methodology section has 4 subsections: Problem Definition, Framework Overview, Technical Details, Training Procedure
Problem Definition includes symbol definitions and formal objective
Framework Overview provides high-level intuition before technical details
Technical Details section shows how to structure complex components
Sample equation/pseudocode formatting provided
Guidance on when to use tables vs text for symbol definitions
Algorithm pseudocode template provided (or example)
Checklist for Methodology quality is included
Guidance on reproduceability (what details are needed?)
Explanation of notation consistency requirements

evals.name: Generate Experiments section framework
evals.prompt: I need to structure my Experiments section. I have results on 5 continuous control benchmarks comparing against 3 baselines. I also have an ablation study and some visualization of learned behaviors. Walk me through the structure and what to include in each part.
evals.expected_output: Structured Experiments section with subsections for Setup, Main Results, Ablation, and Analysis, with guidance on what belongs in each part and how to present results
expectations: Experiments section has 4 subsections: Setup, Main Results, Ablation, Analysis
Setup subsection includes guidance on describing environments, baselines, metrics, and implementation details
Setup guidance clarifies what details are reproducibility-critical
Main Results section explains how to structure tables and narrative interpretation
Ablation subsection explains what to vary and how to interpret results
Analysis section provides options (case studies, visualizations, failure modes)
Guidance on error bars and statistical significance
Recommendations for number of runs (3+) and random seeds
Checklist for experimental rigor provided
Examples of how to quantify results (percentages, not 'significant')
